mechanism_narrative: A low and stable federal funds rate indicates accommodative monetary policy, reducing the cost of capital for businesses and encouraging investment, which translates to positive sentiment and higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The correlation between FEDFUNDS levels and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is consistently negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.1) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate, specifically a rise above a critical threshold, signals an impending economic slowdown or recession, leading to decreased corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points from its 12-month low
evidence_refs: fact:historical recessions often follow unemployment spikes
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with robust housing starts indicate broad-based consumer and investment demand, suggesting a healthy economic expansion. This dual strength boosts corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 5% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 10%
failure_condition: Periods where both RSAFS and HOUST show strong growth do not consistently lead to positive S&P 500 20-day returns, with an average return below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, any acceleration in consumer prices signals increased pressure on the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, which negatively impacts equity valuations due to higher discount rates and recession fears, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed's dual mandate includes price stability
failure_condition: During periods where CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3%, a monthly increase greater than 0.4% does not result in negative S&P 500 5-day returns in at least 60% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing and overall supply-side constraints or demand destruction, leading to lower corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month average growth rate falls below -0.5%
failure_condition: When INDPRO 3-month average growth rate is below -0.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time over a 7-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in real GDP reflects a healthy and expanding economy, which underpins corporate profitability and investor confidence, leading to sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates over any 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Robust non-farm payroll growth combined with a low and stable unemployment rate signifies a strong labor market, boosting consumer spending and corporate revenues, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the immediate 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k and UNRATE is below 4.0%, the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 35% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic uncertainty or recession, a sharp decline in retail sales indicates a significant contraction in consumer spending, which severely impacts corporate earnings and leads to prolonged negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In a recessionary environment, a 3-month annualized decline in RSAFS below -3% does not consistently precede negative S&P 500 60-day returns, with an average return greater than -1%.
